steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a sentence boundary (punctuation) question. The blank falls after "Mission STS-53" and before "seven days and seven hours, the mission ended..." The second part is an independent clause, so the blank must correctly close the first sentence and open the second.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘STS-53, lasting’: Creates a comma splice — two independent clauses joined only by a comma. The construction "...commencing Mission STS-53, lasting seven days..., the mission ended" runs both clauses together illegally. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘STS-53, it lasted’: Also a comma splice — "it lasted" is another independent clause, and a comma alone cannot join two independent clauses. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘STS-53 lasting’: Omits any separator, fusing the two clauses without punctuation. "Lasting" without a comma or period dangles, leaving the first sentence without a proper boundary. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘STS-53. Lasting’: The period ends the first independent clause. "Lasting seven days and seven hours" then becomes a participial phrase correctly introducing the second sentence, whose subject-verb is "the mission ended." ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
